index.title: Dream Catcher
index.for_he: למעבר לממשק בעברית, יש ללחוץ כאן
index.for_ar: للانتقال إلى الواجهة العربية، اضغط هنا
index.desc:
We invite you to tell us about a dream you had in the past year. A meaningful, big dream that deeply affected you. 
All dreams are collected anonymously in the Dream Catcher archive, and together create a collective subconscious map, from which you can view your personal dream.
index.more_info:
"Atara" (Aramaic: place) is a multidisciplinary art project that deals with the collective subconscious that merges our personal dreams with ancient and modern dreams and memories. The project brings together artists, psychoanalysts, and technology entrepreneurs in order to create a place where we can explore our consciousness as individuals and as a group, and draw the boundaries of the social dream in which we live.
The project draws its inspiration from the "Social Dream Matrix", a scientific method invented in 1982 in London by Gordon Lawrence and is used today in many countries around the world. The method was born following the book of Charlotte Beradt, who collected dreams for six years, during the 1930s, when there was anxiety about the totalitarian regime, and which "predicted" some of the events of the war.

form.when: When did you dream the dream?
form.good_bad_dream_label: In your opinion, was it a good or bad dream?
form.association: Can you provide some associations regarding the dream?
form.title: Dream Catcher
form.anonymize: Would you like to remain anonymous?
form.anonymize_small: (Your name will not be published in the database)
form.form_intro: Here are some questions to help us categorize your dream in the archive.
form.legal:
Any dream collected in the system will be uploaded to the dream archive and from there to our mapping system for research and artistic use only by dream researchers and artists. 
The system is an artificial intelligence system that reads the content of the dream and maps it in relation to all the dreams in the system. 

For technical reasons, it is possible that the recorded dreams will undergo sound and text manipulations such as: word cutting, speed changes, volume changes, effect manipulations, etc., to adapt them to the sound format of the archive and the exhibition. 

Some of the recorded dreams received may be filtered and then processed and edited by a team of musicians and sound artists, and used as musical episodes in the exhibition. Later, the edited dreams may be uploaded to additional platforms. If you do not want your dream to be used by artists or researchers or if you have special instructions regarding the use of the dream, please indicate this in the form.
form.legal_confirm: I confirm that the dream does not include content that infringes the rights of third parties, including copyright infringement, invasion of privacy, defamation, etc.
